intro: Pycnarrhena poilanei, also known as the poilane's vine, is a perennial vine native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to exhibit potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pycnarrhena poilanei include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pycnarrhena poilanei is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
